codeReview.summary: The feature adds provenance fields and broad test coverage, but provider-level provenance metadata is computed as the single highest-confidence row while totals still aggregate mixed-confidence rows. This can mislabel mixed exact+estimated aggregates as fully exact, violating the provenance-reporting contract intent.

issues.description: Provider aggregate provenance is derived from a dominant row (`dominantConfidence`/`dominantMethod`) instead of reflecting mixed-confidence composition. Because totals (`totalCost`, `totalTokens`) sum all rows, a provider with any exact row plus estimated rows is labeled `.exact`, which can misrepresent fallback-derived contributions.

`ProviderSummary.hasEstimatedData` is inferred only from `provenanceConfidence`. With the dominant-row aggregation above, mixed datasets can return `false` even when estimated rows are included in totals, so consumers cannot reliably distinguish exact vs fallback-derived aggregate values.
The same dominant-row provenance logic is duplicated in `UsageStore.makeProviderSummaries`, so direct usage-store summary paths inherit the same aggregate provenance mislabeling behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but the required context-read evidence discipline in the token-accounting skill was not satisfied in handoff verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file `.../handoffs/2026-04-09T14-31-59-020Z__m4-reporting-provenance-audit-and-filter-parity__1ce59740-7176-405e-9faa-e932a6791008.json` includes only test and `git rev-parse` commands under `verification.commandsRun`; no command evidence showing required context reads despite SKILL.md requiring that evidence when `followedProcedure=true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill prescribes TDD red/green (add failing scoped tests first), but transcript order shows implementation edits before adding new tests.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking aggregate-provenance correctness issue: provider summaries can report exact confidence for mixed exact+estimated totals. Report written with actionable file/line findings and skill/shared-state observations.
